examples.title: 4-to-2 Binary Encoder
examples.description: Simple binary encoder implementation assuming single active input
examples.code:
ALGORITHM BinaryEncoder
INPUTS: D[0..3]  // 4 input lines
OUTPUTS: Y[0..1] // 2 output lines
BEGIN
  IF D[0] = 1 THEN
    Y[1] ← 0, Y[0] ← 0    // Output: 00
  ELSE IF D[1] = 1 THEN
    Y[1] ← 0, Y[0] ← 1    // Output: 01
  ELSE IF D[2] = 1 THEN
    Y[1] ← 1, Y[0] ← 0    // Output: 10
  ELSE IF D[3] = 1 THEN
    Y[1] ← 1, Y[0] ← 1    // Output: 11
  ELSE
    Y[1] ← 0, Y[0] ← 0    // Output: 00 (no input)
  END IF
END
examples.explanation: This algorithm implements a basic 4-to-2 binary encoder. It checks each input in sequence and produces corresponding binary output. Limitation: incorrect output if multiple inputs are HIGH.

examples.title: 4-to-2 Priority Encoder Implementation
examples.description: Python implementation of priority encoder handling multiple inputs
examples.code:
def priority_encoder(inputs):
    # inputs: list of 4 bits [D0, D1, D2, D3]
    # D3 has highest priority
    # returns: [Y1, Y0, V] where V=1 if valid input
    
    if inputs[3] == 1:    # Highest priority
        return [1, 1, 1]
    elif inputs[2] == 1:
        return [1, 0, 1]
    elif inputs[1] == 1:
        return [0, 1, 1]
    elif inputs[0] == 1:
        return [0, 0, 1]
    else:
        return [0, 0, 0]   # No valid input

# Test cases
print(priority_encoder([0, 0, 0, 1]))  # Output: [1, 1, 1]
print(priority_encoder([0, 0, 1, 0]))  # Output: [1, 0, 1]
print(priority_encoder([0, 1, 0, 0]))  # Output: [0, 1, 1]
print(priority_encoder([1, 0, 0, 0]))  # Output: [0, 0, 1]
print(priority_encoder([0, 0, 0, 0]))  # Output: [0, 0, 0]
examples.explanation: Priority encoder handles multiple simultaneous inputs by checking from highest priority (D3) to lowest (D0). First found active input determines output. V output indicates if any input was active.

examples.title: 8-to-3 Priority Encoder in C
examples.description: C program implementing 8-to-3 priority encoder with highest priority to I7
examples.explanation: 8-to-3 priority encoder where I7 has highest priority. Checks inputs from highest to lowest index. When I5 is active, outputs 101 (binary 5). V=1 indicates valid input.

examples.title: Decimal to BCD Encoder
examples.description: Java implementation of decimal to BCD encoder with 10 inputs to 4 outputs
examples.explanation: Decimal-to-BCD encoder converts 10 decimal inputs to 4-bit BCD output. For input key 5, outputs 0101 (BCD code for 5). Each BCD output bit is generated by ORing specific input lines.
